findings.description: mask_middle with visible=0 hits value[-0:] which is value[:] so the full plaintext is appended after the mask (mask_middle('secret',visible=0) -> '******secret'), a total leak in a function whose entire job is to not leak the value.

The guard len(value)<=visible*2 only catches empty strings when visible=0, so every non-empty value falls through into the leaking branch — visible=0 ('mask everything') is an ordinary caller choice, not an exotic edge case.

mask_char is never validated; a multi-character mask_char inflates the output and the visible+middle structure leaks the exact secret length, making this masking primitive self-defeating for its stated anti-leak purpose.

truncate counts code points rather than grapheme clusters, so emoji/combining-mark input is miscounted and the slice text[:limit-len(suffix)] can sever a multi-code-point cluster, contradicting the docstring's clean 'character' contract.
slugify does slug[:max_length].rstrip('-') so it can return shorter-than-max and never validates max_length — a negative value makes slug[:-n] silently chop the tail instead of erroring.
pluralize's naive singular+'s' fallback silently mangles every irregular noun (person/people, child/children) with no signal to the caller; documented as 'naive' but still a correctness trap.
indent_block treats whitespace-only lines as empty via line.strip() and leaves them un-indented/unchanged, an undocumented behavioral choice that surprises callers expecting uniform indentation.
